title: Alpha content parity and dead-copy prune
summary: Prune stale promises and align all live content surfaces with the completed alpha arc.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: No public or in-game copy promises unavailable systems; all five biomes feel represented in alpha documentation.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Sync public docs and exposed commands with actual build/test/validate behavior after packaging fixes.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Remove stale or redundant copy paths and ensure five-biome parity in notebook-facing surfaces.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Update screenshot/contact-sheet docs for the representative five-biome alpha state.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Ensure route/support documentation matches the actual Route v2 flow and no legacy request language leaks.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

finding: The package scripts already expose build, test, science source-ledger checking, agent validation, review-drop packaging, and review-drop verification.
The dedicated review-drop checklist already describes the clean-extract flow and the science-check gate.

README.md is the remaining public entrypoint drift: it omits science:check, review:pack, and review:verify from the useful-command list and still uses a generic archive-sharing note instead of the verified source-drop workflow.
main_scope: Update README.md so Useful commands names npm run science:check, npm run review:pack, and npm run review:verify -- <archive.tgz>.
Separate fresh local verification from source review-drop verification in README.md.
Replace the generic node_modules archive note with a pointer to docs/review-drop-checklist.md and the review:pack/review:verify flow.
Leave docs/review-drop-checklist.md, package.json, scripts/create-review-drop.mjs, and scripts/verify-review-drop.mjs behavior unchanged unless a direct mismatch is found while editing.
non_goals: No runtime code, route behavior, station state, save schema, test behavior, package script behavior, authored science/content, dated historical report, alpha promise, season-three or biome-six language, release date, direct API mode, crafting, combat, inventory, account, or planner UI change.

result: Added science:check, review:pack, and review:verify to README.md's useful command list.
Separated fresh local verification from source review-drop clean-extract verification in README.md.
Replaced the generic archive-sharing note with the dedicated review-drop pack/verify workflow and a pointer to docs/review-drop-checklist.md.
scope_notes: Left package.json scripts, docs/review-drop-checklist.md, scripts/create-review-drop.mjs, scripts/verify-review-drop.mjs, runtime behavior, tests, save schema, authored content, and historical reports unchanged.

findings: README.md now exposes the actual science-check and review-drop commands.
README.md's clean-extract verification description matches scripts/verify-review-drop.mjs.
The pass stayed docs-only and did not change package scripts, verifier behavior, runtime code, tests, save schema, authored content, historical reports, or future-scope promises.

finding: README.md still describes the filed alpha arc as having request cues after filing.
docs/architecture.md still leads the live loop section with field-request/request wording instead of Route v2 outing language.
docs/content-authoring.md lacks compact author guidance for Route v2/support wording, leaving room for quest, checklist, loadout, or planner promises to drift back in.
main_scope: Update the README alpha-arc bullet so it names route/support/notebook cues instead of generic request cues.

Refresh docs/architecture.md prose around the progression section so player-facing language leads with Route v2 outings, field-season routes, notebook filing, replay labels, and the tiny support slot while keeping field-request module/save names accurate.
Add one short Route v2 and support copy guidance section to docs/content-authoring.md.
Keep the pass docs-only unless a direct typo in a doc link requires a tiny correction.
non_goals: No runtime code, route definitions, support behavior, save schema, tests, station layout, station state, authored science facts, content roster, geometry, packaging scripts, review-drop workflow, historical report rewrites, code-module renames, save-field renames, lane-1 station copy, lane-2 science/content copy, or lane-3 screenshot/proof docs.
verification: rg -n "request cues|routes and requests|Field requests and guided season flow|quest|checklist|loadout|planner" README.md docs/architecture.md docs/content-authoring.md

Added compact Route v2/support copy guidance to docs/content-authoring.md warning against quest, checklist, loadout, planner, inventory, or extra-support-slot promises.
Tightened the existing observationPrompt copy-budget wording from question to noticing prompt so the route/support scan no longer catches a generic quest substring.
